name: Skillet Caramel Apple Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine 1 cup sugar and water in a heavy, deep, oven-proof 10-inch skillet. Bring to boil over medium heat, stirring occasionally.
2. Cook until light caramel stage or pale amber color, stirring occasionally. Remove from heat.
3. Swirl in butter and 1/2 teaspoon almond extract. Set aside.
4. Peel, core, and cut each apple into 4 wedges. Arrange about 3/4 of the apples in a tight single layer over the caramel.
5. Cut remaining apple wedges in halves and place atop the first apple layer. Cover skillet and cook over medium-low heat until apples release juices, about 10 minutes.
6. Uncover skillet, increase heat to medium, and continue cooking about 25-30 minutes, or until caramel syrup is thick but not burnt (adjust heat if caramel starts cooking too fast).
7. Baste apples occasionally with caramel syrup using a baster or spoon. Remove from heat and let cool.
8. Sift cake flour with baking powder and 1/2 cup plus 2 tablespoons sugar in a bowl.
9. Make well in the center of this mixture.
10. In another bowl, whisk egg, apple juice, and remaining 2 tablespoons sugar, then beat until stiff but not dry.
11. Fold a small amount of batter into egg whites, then fold the mixture back into the remaining batter.
12. Pour over apples in skillet. Bake at 350°F for 25-30 minutes, or until a wooden pick inserted in the center comes out clean.
13. Let cool for 5 minutes. Loosen cake around edges.
14. Place platter over skillet and carefully invert pan, shaking gently to release cake into platter. Spoon any remaining glaze over cake.
15. Serve with whipped topping. Makes 12 servings.
